title: fire_drought_conditions
description: A wildfire ignites during a severe multi-year drought, creating extreme fire behavior and depleted water resources.

timeline.title: Fire Ignition in Drought Zone
timeline.description: Fire starts in an area under exceptional drought. All vegetation is critically dry.

timeline.title: Extreme Fire Behavior
timeline.description: Fire behavior analyst reports crown fire potential and extreme rate of spread. Escalate resources.

timeline.title: Water Supply Deficit
timeline.description: Local water tanks and hydrants have reduced pressure due to drought. Identify alternate sources.

timeline.title: Multi-Agency Response
timeline.description: Mutual aid activated. Additional engines and water tenders from neighboring jurisdictions en route.

timeline.title: Precautionary Evacuations Expanded
timeline.description: Drought conditions mean fire could reignite easily. Expand evacuation zones proactively.

timeline.title: Temporary Containment
timeline.description: Fire temporarily contained. Drought conditions require extended mop-up and monitoring.

node-1.title: Drought-Condition Fire Ignition
node-1.situation: A wildfire has ignited during the region's third year of severe drought. Fuel moisture content is at record lows. Fire behavior is already extreme within the first 15 minutes of ignition.
node-1.question: How do you calibrate your response given drought conditions?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately request the highest level of mutual aid and escalate resource orders, anticipating extreme behavior.

options.label: B
options.text: Respond with standard initial attack resources and reassess once the fire size is confirmed.

options.label: C
options.text: Stage resources at a distance and wait for a break in fire behavior before committing.

node-2.title: Water Supply Crisis
node-2.situation: Fire engines are reporting critically low water pressure at local hydrants due to drought-related low reservoir levels. Two tenders have run dry and the nearest refill point is 20 miles away.
node-2.question: How do you manage water supply logistics?

options.label: A
options.text: Establish a water shuttle with multiple tenders, identify all nearby water sources including ponds and swimming pools.

options.label: B
options.text: Reduce suppression activities to conserve water and wait for mutual aid tenders to arrive.

node-3.title: Crown Fire Potential
node-3.situation: The fire behavior analyst confirms crown fire potential in a stand of drought-stressed trees ahead. Sending crews into this area would be extremely dangerous.
node-3.question: What do you do with crews positioned near the crown fire zone?

options.label: A
options.text: Pull crews back to a safe distance and use air tankers to suppress the crown fire potential before ground reengagement.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep crews in place as the crown fire potential is not yet confirmed.

node-4.title: Extended Mop-Up Challenge
node-4.situation: The fire is temporarily contained but drought conditions mean deep-seated burning in root systems could cause reignition. Standard mop-up will not be sufficient.
node-4.question: What mop-up strategy do you implement?

options.label: A
options.text: Order extensive mop-up to a cold-out standard, increase patrol frequency, and keep evacuation zones active.

options.label: B
options.text: Declare the fire contained and begin releasing resources to reduce costs.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: Extended mop-up operations have confirmed the fire is cold. Patrol continues due to ongoing drought conditions. No reignition events have occurred.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
